effect.en: a change that happens because of something else

ex.en: The medicine had a strong effect on her headache.
ex.ko: 그 약은 그녀의 두통에 강한 효과가 있었다.

effect.en: cause something to happen, especially a change

ex.en: The new manager effected several changes in the department.
ex.ko: 새 관리자는 부서에 여러 변화를 일으켰다.

employ.en: pay someone to work for you

ex.en: The factory employs over 200 workers.
ex.ko: 그 공장은 200명이 넘는 근로자를 고용하고 있다.

employ.en: use a particular method, skill, or object for a purpose

ex.en: The artist employed bold colors in her work.
ex.ko: 그 화가는 자신의 작품에 대담한 색을 활용했다.

employ.en: the state of having a paid job with someone

ex.en: He has been in the employ of the same company for ten years.
We don't have enough time.
Is the soup hot enough for you?

enough.en: an amount that is sufficient for what is needed

ex.en: She has saved enough to buy a new car.
ex.ko: 그녀는 새 차를 살 만큼 충분한 돈을 모았다.

essay.en: a short piece of writing about a particular subject

ex.en: She wrote an essay about climate change.
ex.ko: 그녀는 기후 변화에 관한 에세이를 썼다.

essay.en: attempt to do something, especially something difficult (formal)

ex.en: He essayed a smile despite the pain.
Sleep is essential for good health.

essential.en: a thing that is absolutely necessary

ex.en: Pack only the essentials for the trip.

evidence.en: facts or information that show something is true

ex.en: The police found no evidence of a crime.

evidence.en: show clearly that something is true

ex.en: Her success evidences years of hard work.

excess.en: an amount that is more than what is needed or allowed

ex.en: He was charged extra for excess baggage.
ex.ko: 그는 초과 수하물 때문에 추가 요금을 냈다.

excess.en: more than the usual, necessary, or allowed amount

ex.en: Passengers must pay extra for excess baggage.
ex.ko: 승객들은 초과 수하물에 대해 추가 요금을 내야 한다.

exhibit.en: show something in a public place for people to see

ex.en: The museum exhibits ancient artifacts from Egypt.
ex.ko: 그 박물관은 이집트 고대 유물을 전시한다.

exhibit.en: show a particular quality, feeling, or ability

ex.en: The patient exhibited signs of improvement.

exhibit.en: an object or collection of objects shown to the public

ex.en: The dinosaur exhibit was the most popular part of the museum.
ex.ko: 공룡 전시품은 그 박물관에서 가장 인기 있는 부분이었다.

exile.cue: 조국에서 쫓겨나 다른 나라에 사는 상태
exile.en: the state of being forced to live away from one's own country, often for political reasons

ex.en: The former president died in exile.
ex.ko: 그 전직 대통령은 망명 생활 중 사망했다.

exile.en: a person who lives away from their home country, often unable to return

ex.en: Many exiles returned home after the war ended.
ex.ko: 전쟁이 끝난 후 많은 망명자들이 고국으로 돌아갔다.

exile.en: force someone to leave their own country, often for political reasons

ex.en: The dictator exiled his political rivals.

exploit.en: treat someone unfairly by using their work or situation to your own advantage

ex.en: The company was accused of exploiting its workers.
ex.ko: 그 회사는 노동자들을 착취했다는 비난을 받았다.

exploit.en: use something fully in order to gain an advantage from it

ex.en: The country hopes to exploit its natural resources.
The book describes the explorer's daring exploits.
ex.ko: 그 책은 그 탐험가의 대담한 위업들을 기록한다.

express.en: show your thoughts or feelings through words or actions

ex.en: She expressed her thanks to the whole team.

express.cue: 정거장을 거의 서지 않는 빠른 교통편
express.en: traveling quickly and stopping at few or no stations

ex.en: We took the express train to save time.
I sent the package express to make sure it arrived on time.
ex.ko: 제시간에 도착하도록 그 소포를 속달로 보냈다.

extreme.en: very great in degree; far beyond what is normal

ex.en: They faced extreme weather conditions on the trip.
ex.ko: 그들은 그 여행에서 극도의 기상 상황을 겪었다.

extreme.en: the highest or furthest possible degree of something

ex.en: He went from one extreme to the other.
ex.ko: 그는 한 극단에서 다른 극단으로 치달았다.

eye.en: the body part used for seeing

ex.en: She has beautiful blue eyes.
ex.ko: 그녀는 아름다운 파란 눈을 가지고 있다.

eye.en: look at someone or something carefully

ex.en: She eyed the stranger suspiciously.

face-to-face.en: involving direct personal contact rather than phone or online

ex.en: We prefer face-to-face meetings over video calls.
ex.ko: 우리는 화상 통화보다 대면 회의를 선호한다.

face-to-face.en: in person, in the same place, not by phone or online

ex.en: Let's talk face-to-face instead of texting.

factor.en: something that influences or contributes to a result

ex.en: Diet is an important factor in staying healthy.
ex.ko: 식단은 건강을 유지하는 데 중요한 요인이다.

factor.en: a number that divides exactly into another number

ex.en: 2 and 3 are factors of 6.

factor.en: include something as part of your planning or calculation

ex.en: We need to factor in the cost of shipping.
The teacher was fair to all the students.

fair.en: an event where people buy and sell things or where rides and games are set up

ex.en: We went to the county fair last weekend.
ex.ko: 우리는 지난 주말에 지역 축제에 갔다.

fair.en: (of hair or skin) light in color

ex.en: She has fair skin and blonde hair.
ex.ko: 그녀는 흰 피부와 금발 머리를 가지고 있다.

ex.en: The forecast promises fair weather this weekend.
ex.ko: 일기예보에서 이번 주말은 맑은 날씨라고 한다.

fair.en: make the surface of something smooth and even, especially the hull of a boat or an aircraft body

ex.en: The workers faired the hull before painting it.
ex.ko: 작업자들은 페인트칠 전에 선체를 매끄럽게 다듬었다.

faithful.en: loyal and never letting someone down

ex.en: He remained faithful to his wife.

faithful.en: the loyal followers of a religion (used with 'the')

ex.en: The faithful gathered at the temple for the ceremony.

fake.en: not genuine; made to look like the real thing

ex.en: She was carrying a fake designer bag.
ex.ko: 그녀는 가짜 명품 가방을 들고 있었다.

fake.en: pretend to have a feeling, illness, or quality that you do not really have

ex.en: He faked an injury to avoid playing.
ex.ko: 그는 경기에 나가지 않으려고 부상을 꾸며냈다.

fake.en: something that is not genuine but made to look real

ex.en: The painting turned out to be a fake.

fan.en: someone who likes and admires a person, team, or activity very much

ex.en: He is a huge fan of that soccer team.

fan.en: a machine with blades that turns to create a current of air

ex.en: Turn on the fan, it's too hot in here.
ex.ko: 선풍기 좀 켜, 여기 너무 더워.

fan.en: move air with a fan or something flat, to cool someone or make a fire burn better

ex.en: She fanned herself with a magazine in the heat.
ex.ko: 그녀는 더위 속에서 잡지로 부채질을 했다.

fare.en: the money you pay to travel on a bus, train, or taxi

ex.en: The bus fare went up this year.

fare.en: do well or badly in a particular situation

ex.en: How did you fare in the exam?

farther.en: at or to a greater distance (comparative of far)

ex.en: We need to walk a bit farther to reach the lake.
ex.ko: 호수에 도착하려면 조금 더 멀리 걸어야 한다.

ex.en: The farther bank of the river was covered in trees.
ex.ko: 강 건너편의 더 먼 둑은 나무로 덮여 있었다.

fashion.en: the popular style of clothes or behavior at a certain time

ex.en: She works in the fashion industry.

fashion.en: make or shape something, often using your hands or simple tools

ex.en: He fashioned a small boat out of wood.

fast.en: moving or able to move quickly

ex.en: He drives a very fast car.
She ran fast to catch the bus.

fast.en: eat no food for a period of time

ex.en: Some people fast during religious holidays.
ex.ko: 어떤 사람들은 종교 명절 동안 금식한다.

fast.en: a period of time when you eat no food

ex.en: He broke his fast at sunset.
My father works at a hospital.

father.en: become the father of a child

ex.en: He fathered three children before he turned thirty.
ex.ko: 그는 서른이 되기 전에 세 아이의 아버지가 되었다.

fault.en: the responsibility for something bad that happened

ex.en: It's not your fault that the train was late.
ex.ko: 기차가 늦은 건 네 잘못이 아니야.

fault.en: a problem or weakness in something

ex.en: The mechanic found a fault in the engine.

fault.en: criticize or blame someone for a mistake

ex.en: You can't fault her effort.

feast.cue: 많은 사람이 모여 먹는 큰 식사
feast.en: a large meal, usually to celebrate something special

ex.en: The wedding feast lasted late into the night.

feast.en: eat a large and enjoyable meal

ex.en: They feasted on turkey and pie for hours.
ex.ko: 그들은 몇 시간 동안 칠면조와 파이로 포식했다.

feather.en: one of the light, soft things that cover a bird's body

ex.en: A bright feather fell from the bird's wing.
ex.ko: 밝은색 깃털 하나가 새의 날개에서 떨어졌다.

ex.en: The chicks feathered within a few weeks of hatching.
ex.ko: 그 새끼 새들은 부화한 지 몇 주 만에 깃털이 났다.

fence.en: a barrier made of wood or wire that surrounds an area

ex.en: They built a fence around the garden.

fence.en: put a fence around an area to enclose it

ex.en: They fenced the yard to keep the dog in.
ex.ko: 그들은 개를 가두려고 마당에 울타리를 쳤다.

field.en: a piece of open land, often used for crops or animals

ex.en: Cows were grazing in the field.

field.en: an area of ground used for playing a sport

ex.en: The players ran onto the field.

field.en: a particular area of study or work

ex.en: She is an expert in the field of medicine.

field.en: deal with questions from a group of people, answering them one by one

ex.en: The spokesperson fielded questions from reporters.
She turned fifteen last week.
There are fifteen students in this class.
ex.ko: 이 반에는 학생이 열다섯 명 있다.

ex.en: There were fifty people at the party.
The bridge is fifty meters long.

fighting.en: the act of physically struggling or battling

ex.en: Fighting broke out between the two groups.
The troops were in fighting condition.

fill.en: make something full, or become full

ex.en: He filled the glass with water.

fill.cue: 배부르게 먹을 만큼의 양 (eat one's fill)
fill.en: enough of something to satisfy you completely

ex.en: We ate our fill at the buffet.

finger.en: one of the five parts at the end of the hand

ex.en: She pointed her finger at the map.

finger.en: touch or feel something with your fingers

ex.en: She fingered the fabric to check its quality.
ex.ko: 그녀는 원단의 질을 확인하려고 손가락으로 만져보았다.

firm.en: solid and not easily moved, pressed, or changed

ex.en: He gave her hand a firm handshake.

firm.en: a business organization, especially one providing professional services

ex.en: She works for a law firm downtown.
ex.ko: 그녀는 시내에 있는 법률 회사에서 일한다.

ex.en: Her muscles firmed up after months of training.
ex.ko: 그녀의 근육은 몇 달간의 운동 후 단단해졌다.

firm.en: in a firm, determined way, without giving in

ex.en: She stood firm against the pressure to quit.

first.en: coming before all others in order or time

ex.en: This is my first visit to Korea.
First, wash your hands.

first.en: the beginning or earliest part of something

ex.en: At first, I didn't understand the instructions.
She has five brothers.
ex.ko: 그녀는 남자 형제가 다섯 명 있다.

ex.en: Five apples were left in the basket.
ex.ko: 바구니에 사과 다섯 개가 남아 있었다.

flag.en: a piece of cloth with a design that represents a country or group

ex.en: The flag waved in the wind.

flag.en: become weaker or less active over time

ex.en: His enthusiasm began to flag after the first hour.
ex.ko: 그의 열정은 한 시간이 지나자 시들해지기 시작했다.

flap.en: move up and down or from side to side, especially in the wind

ex.en: The flag flapped in the strong wind.

flap.en: a flat piece attached along one edge that can be lifted or moved, such as on an envelope or pocket

ex.en: He tucked the flap of the envelope inside.
ex.ko: 그는 봉투의 덮개를 안쪽으로 접어 넣었다.

fleet.en: a group of ships, vehicles, or aircraft owned or controlled by the same organization

ex.en: The company has a fleet of delivery trucks.
ex.ko: 그 회사는 배송 트럭 선단을 보유하고 있다.

ex.en: The deer were fleet and hard to catch.

flip.en: turn something over quickly with a sudden movement

ex.en: He flipped the pancake in the pan.

flip.en: turn the pages of a book or magazine quickly

ex.en: She flipped through the magazine while waiting.

flip.en: a quick, sudden movement, especially turning something over

ex.en: With a flip of her wrist, she tossed the coin.
ex.ko: 그녀는 손목을 휙 움직여 동전을 던졌다.

flower.en: the colorful part of a plant that often has a pleasant smell

ex.en: She gave him a bunch of flowers.
ex.ko: 그녀는 그에게 꽃 한 다발을 주었다.

ex.en: These roses flower from June to September.

flutter.en: a quick, light movement, especially of wings or something thin

ex.en: There was a flutter of wings as the bird flew away.
She felt a flutter of excitement before the interview.

flutter.en: move the wings quickly up and down

ex.en: The butterfly fluttered from flower to flower.
ex.ko: 나비가 꽃에서 꽃으로 날개를 파닥이며 날아다녔다.

foot.en: the part of the leg below the ankle that you stand on

ex.en: She hurt her foot while running.

foot.en: a unit of length equal to 12 inches (about 30 cm)

ex.en: The ceiling is about ten feet high.

foot.en: the lowest part of something such as a hill or a set of stairs

ex.en: They set up camp at the foot of the mountain.

foot.en: pay for something, especially a bill

ex.en: His parents footed the bill for his education.
My father is forty years old.
Forty guests attended the wedding.

forward.en: toward the front or in the direction someone is facing

ex.en: She leaned forward to hear better.
ex.ko: 그녀는 더 잘 들으려고 앞으로 몸을 기울였다.

forward.en: send a message or email on to another person

ex.en: Can you forward me that email?

forward.en: directed or moving toward the front

ex.en: The car has a forward-facing camera.
ex.ko: 그 차는 전방을 향한 카메라가 있다.

foul.en: an action in a sport that breaks the rules

ex.en: The referee called a foul on the player.

foul.en: having a strong, unpleasant smell or taste

ex.en: There was a foul smell coming from the trash can.

foul.en: break the rules of a sport, especially by touching or hitting another player unfairly

ex.en: The defender fouled the striker inside the box.
ex.ko: 그 수비수는 페널티 박스 안에서 공격수에게 반칙을 범했다.

found.en: start or create an organization, company, or institution

ex.en: The company was founded in 1995.

found.en: discovered by chance, especially something lost by someone else

ex.en: She checked the found-items list at the station.

found.en: food and a place to stay given to a worker in addition to wages (old-fashioned)

ex.en: Farmhands were once paid in wages and found.
ex.ko: 예전에 농장 일꾼들은 임금과 숙식 제공으로 보수를 받았다.

ex.en: There are four seasons in a year.
Four chairs were around the table.
ex.ko: 테이블 주위에 의자 네 개가 있었다.

ex.en: She will be fourteen next month.
ex.ko: 그녀는 다음 달에 열네 살이 된다.

ex.en: She has fourteen cousins.

freak.en: become extremely upset, frightened, or excited

ex.en: She freaked out when she saw the spider.
ex.ko: 그녀는 거미를 보고 겁에 질려 흥분했다.

freak.cue: 무언가에 광적으로 몰두하는 사람 (a fitness freak)
freak.en: a person who is extremely enthusiastic about or obsessed with something (informal)

ex.en: My brother is a total fitness freak.
It's freezing outside, so wear a coat.

freezing.en: the process of a liquid becoming solid because of cold

ex.en: The freezing of the lake usually happens in December.
There are frequent delays on this train line.
ex.ko: 이 기차 노선에는 잦은 지연이 있다.

ex.en: Writers used to frequent that old café.
ex.ko: 작가들은 그 오래된 카페를 자주 드나들었다.

fresh.en: recently made, picked, or produced; not old

ex.en: We bought some fresh vegetables at the market.
ex.ko: 우리는 시장에서 신선한 채소를 좀 샀다.

fresh.en: clean and pleasant, or new and different

ex.en: Let's go outside and get some fresh air.
ex.ko: 밖에 나가서 상쾌한 공기를 좀 마시자.

ex.en: He was fresh out of college when he got the job.
ex.ko: 그는 대학을 갓 졸업했을 때 그 일자리를 얻었다.

front.en: the part of something that faces forward

ex.en: Let's meet in front of the station.

front.en: located at the front of something

ex.en: Please use the front door.
The hotel fronts the beach.

full.en: holding as much as possible; with no empty space left

ex.en: The bus was full, so we had to stand.
ex.ko: 버스가 만원이어서 우리는 서 있어야 했다.

full.cue: 정통으로, 완전히 (hit full in the face)

ex.en: The ball hit him full in the face.

fume.en: strong-smelling or harmful smoke or gas

ex.en: Car fumes pollute the air in big cities.

fume.en: be very angry, often without saying so directly

ex.en: She fumed silently after the meeting.

further.en: to a greater degree or extent

ex.en: We need to discuss this further.
ex.ko: 우리는 이것에 대해 더 논의할 필요가 있다.

ex.en: Please contact us for further information.
ex.ko: 추가 정보를 원하시면 저희에게 연락해 주세요.

further.en: help something to develop or succeed

ex.en: She donated money to further the cause of education.
ex.ko: 그녀는 교육이라는 대의를 촉진하기 위해 돈을 기부했다.

fuss.en: unnecessary excitement or worry about something small

ex.en: Don't make such a fuss over a little mistake.
ex.ko: 작은 실수 하나에 그렇게 야단법석 떨지 마.

fuss.en: worry or become upset about small, unimportant things

ex.en: Stop fussing over every little detail.

garage.en: a building or part of a building where a car is kept

ex.en: He parked the car in the garage.

garage.en: a place where cars are repaired

ex.en: I took my car to the garage to get it fixed.

garage.en: put or keep a vehicle in a garage

ex.en: He garaged the car for the winter.
ex.ko: 그는 겨울 동안 차를 차고에 넣어두었다.

garden.en: an area of land, often near a house, where flowers or vegetables are grown

ex.en: She grows tomatoes in her garden.

garden.en: work in a garden, growing and taking care of plants

ex.en: She likes to garden on weekends.
ex.ko: 그녀는 주말에 정원 가꾸는 것을 좋아한다.

gas.en: a fuel in the form of gas, used for cooking or heating

ex.en: We cook on a gas stove.

gas.en: a substance like air that is neither solid nor liquid

ex.en: Oxygen is a gas that we need to breathe.
ex.ko: 산소는 우리가 숨쉬는 데 필요한 기체이다.

gas.en: (American English) fuel used to make a car engine run

ex.en: We need to stop and get some gas.
ex.ko: 우리는 멈춰서 휘발유를 좀 넣어야 한다.

gas.en: attack or kill someone using poisonous gas

ex.en: Thousands of soldiers were gassed during World War I.
ex.ko: 제1차 세계대전 중 수천 명의 병사들이 독가스 공격을 받았다.

gasp.cue: 놀라거나 힘들어서 숨을 크게 들이쉬는 소리
gasp.en: a short, quick breath taken with the mouth open, from shock or exhaustion

ex.en: She let out a gasp when she saw the surprise party.
ex.ko: 그녀는 깜짝 파티를 보고 숨을 헉 들이쉬었다.

gasp.en: breathe in a quick, noisy way because you are surprised, in pain, or out of breath

ex.en: She gasped for air after the race.

gaze.en: a long, steady look at someone or something

ex.en: He met her gaze across the room.
ex.ko: 그는 방 건너편에서 그녀의 시선과 마주쳤다.

gaze.en: look steadily at something for a long time

ex.en: He gazed out the window, lost in thought.
ex.ko: 그는 생각에 잠긴 채 창밖을 응시했다.

gear.en: a part of a machine, such as a car or bicycle, that controls speed

ex.en: She shifted into a higher gear on the highway.
ex.ko: 그녀는 고속도로에서 더 높은 기어로 변속했다.

gear.en: the equipment needed for a particular activity

ex.en: Don't forget your camping gear.

gear.en: adjust or design something to suit a particular purpose or group

ex.en: The course is geared toward beginners.

general.en: affecting or including most people or things; not specific

ex.en: The general opinion is that prices will rise.
ex.ko: 일반적인 의견은 물가가 오를 것이라는 것이다.

general.en: a very high-ranking officer in the army

ex.en: The general led his troops into battle.
ex.ko: 그 장군은 군대를 이끌고 전투에 나섰다.

general.en: command an army as a general (rare)

ex.en: He generaled the troops through the final campaign.

gentle.en: kind, calm, and not rough in how you treat people

ex.en: She has a very gentle personality.
ex.ko: 그녀는 매우 온화한 성격을 가지고 있다.

ex.en: There was a gentle breeze on the beach.

gentle.en: calm an animal by handling it gently

ex.en: The trainer gentled the frightened horse.

gesture.en: a movement of the hands, arms, or body used to express meaning

ex.en: He made a gesture to show he was joking.
ex.ko: 그는 농담이라는 것을 나타내는 몸짓을 했다.

gesture.en: an action that shows a particular feeling or intention

ex.en: Sending flowers was a kind gesture.
ex.ko: 꽃을 보낸 것은 친절한 마음의 표시였다.

gesture.en: make a movement with your hand or head to express something or show direction

ex.en: She gestured toward the door.

ghost.en: the spirit of a dead person that some people believe can appear

ex.en: The children told ghost stories at the campfire.
ex.ko: 아이들은 모닥불 앞에서 유령 이야기를 했다.

ghost.cue: 다른 사람 이름으로 대신 글을 쓰다
ghost.en: write something, such as a book or speech, for someone else who is credited as the author

ex.en: The celebrity's memoir was ghosted by a professional writer.
ex.ko: 그 유명인의 회고록은 전문 작가가 대필했다.

gift.en: something given to someone, especially on a special occasion

ex.en: Thank you for the gift.

gift.en: give something to someone as a gift

ex.en: She gifted her sister a necklace for her birthday.

give.en: hand something to someone, or let them have it

ex.en: Can you give me a glass of water?
ex.ko: 물 한 잔 줄 수 있어?

give.en: the ability of something to bend or stretch slightly under pressure

ex.en: This mattress has a lot of give.

given.en: already stated, decided, or agreed on

ex.en: At any given time, only ten people can enter.
ex.ko: 정해진 어느 시점이든 열 명만 들어갈 수 있다.

given.en: something that is accepted as true or certain to happen

ex.en: Rising prices are a given in this economy.
ex.ko: 이 경제 상황에서 물가 상승은 당연한 일이다.

glare.en: a strong, unpleasant light that is difficult to look at

ex.en: The glare from the sun made it hard to see the road.
ex.ko: 햇빛의 눈부심 때문에 길이 잘 보이지 않았다.

ex.en: She gave him an angry glare.
He glared at the driver who cut him off.

gleam.en: a small, brief flash of light or shine

ex.en: There was a gleam of excitement in her eyes.

gleam.en: shine with a soft, bright light

ex.en: Her eyes gleamed with excitement.

glimpse.en: a very brief look at something

ex.en: I only caught a glimpse of the singer.
ex.ko: 나는 그 가수를 잠깐 언뜻 봤을 뿐이다.

glimpse.en: see someone or something for a very short time

ex.en: I glimpsed her face in the crowd.
ex.ko: 나는 군중 속에서 그녀의 얼굴을 언뜻 보았다.

glory.en: great honor or fame won by an important achievement

ex.en: The team celebrated their moment of glory.

glory.en: take great pride or pleasure in something

ex.en: The team gloried in their unexpected victory.
ex.ko: 그 팀은 예상치 못한 승리를 만끽했다.

ex.en: This is a good restaurant.
She did it for the good of the team.
ex.ko: 그녀는 팀의 이익을 위해 그렇게 했다.

good.en: well (informal, nonstandard use of 'good' as an adverb)

ex.en: You did real good on that test.
ex.ko: 너 그 시험 정말 잘 봤어.

grace.en: a smooth, beautiful, and elegant quality in movement or manner

ex.en: The dancer moved across the stage with grace.
ex.ko: 그 무용수는 우아하게 무대를 가로질러 움직였다.

grace.en: extra time allowed before a deadline or penalty

ex.en: The bank gave us a 30-day grace period.

grace.en: make something more attractive or special by being present or added to it

ex.en: Her paintings grace the walls of the gallery.
ex.ko: 그녀의 그림들이 그 갤러리의 벽을 장식하고 있다.

graduate.en: a person who has completed a course of study at a school or university

ex.en: She is a graduate of Seoul National University.

graduate.en: complete a course of study and receive a degree

ex.en: He graduated from high school last year.

graduate.en: relating to study for a degree beyond a bachelor's degree

ex.en: She is enrolled in a graduate program in psychology.
ex.ko: 그녀는 심리학 대학원 과정에 등록되어 있다.

graph.en: a diagram showing how numbers or values relate to each other

ex.en: The graph shows sales over the last five years.
The teacher asked us to graph our test scores over time.
ex.ko: 선생님은 우리에게 시험 점수를 시간에 따라 그래프로 나타내라고 했다.

grass.en: a green plant that covers the ground

ex.en: The children played on the grass.

grass.en: inform the police about someone's illegal activities (British informal)

ex.en: He grassed on his friends to avoid punishment.

growing.en: becoming larger, greater, or more in amount

ex.en: There is growing concern about climate change.
ex.ko: 기후 변화에 대한 우려가 커지고 있다.

growing.en: the process of a living thing increasing in size

ex.en: The growing of these plants requires a lot of sunlight.
ex.ko: 이 식물들의 성장에는 많은 햇빛이 필요하다.

half.cue: 둘로 똑같이 나눈 것 중 하나
half.en: one of two equal parts of something

ex.en: Can I have half of your sandwich?
ex.ko: 네 샌드위치 반 나눠 줄 수 있어?

ex.en: The cup is half full.

half.en: being one of two equal parts of something

ex.en: She only ate half a sandwich.

handicap.en: something that makes it harder for someone to succeed or make progress

ex.en: Not speaking the language was a big handicap for him.
ex.ko: 그 언어를 못하는 것은 그에게 큰 불리한 조건이었다.

handicap.cue: 골프 등 경기에서 실력 차 보정
handicap.en: an advantage given to a weaker player to make a competition fairer

ex.en: His golf handicap is twelve.

handicap.en: (somewhat old-fashioned) a physical or mental condition that limits a person's abilities

ex.en: The building was designed for people with a handicap.
ex.ko: 그 건물은 장애가 있는 사람들을 위해 설계되었다.

handicap.en: make it more difficult for someone to succeed by putting them at a disadvantage

ex.en: Lack of funding handicapped the research project.
ex.ko: 자금 부족이 그 연구 프로젝트에 지장을 주었다.

hat.en: something you wear on your head

ex.en: He wore a hat to block the sun.
He hatted himself against the sun before heading out.
ex.ko: 그는 나가기 전 햇빛을 가리려고 모자를 썼다.

headline.en: the title of a newspaper article, printed in large letters

ex.en: The story made the front-page headline.

headline.en: be the main performer at a concert or event

ex.en: The band will headline the summer festival.
ex.ko: 그 밴드가 여름 축제의 헤드라이너로 설 것이다.

ex.en: Come here, please.

here.en: this place, the location where you are now

ex.en: From here, you can see the whole valley.

here.en: present in this place (formal or dialectal)

ex.en: The witness here present can confirm the facts.
ex.ko: 여기 있는 증인이 그 사실을 확인해 줄 수 있다.

hide.en: go somewhere so that you cannot be seen

ex.en: The cat hid under the bed.

hide.en: put something where others cannot find it

ex.en: She hid the present in the closet.

hide.en: the skin of a large animal, especially when used for leather

ex.en: The bag is made from cow hide.
That is a very high mountain.

high.en: at or to a great height

ex.en: The bird flew high in the sky.

high.en: the highest point or level that something reaches

ex.en: Stock prices hit a new high this week.
ex.ko: 이번 주 주가가 새로운 최고치를 기록했다.

ex.en: I stayed home all day.

home.en: to or at the place where you live

ex.en: Let's go home now.

home.en: relating to the place where you live, or (in sports) played at your own team's stadium

ex.en: The home team won the match easily.

homeward.en: moving toward or leading to home

ex.en: They began their homeward journey at sunset.
ex.ko: 그들은 해질 무렵 집으로 향하는 여정을 시작했다.

ex.en: They headed homeward as the sun began to set.
ex.ko: 그들은 해가 지기 시작하자 집으로 향했다.

honey.en: a sweet, sticky substance made by bees

ex.en: She added honey to her tea.

honey.en: a word used to address someone you love

ex.en: Honey, dinner is ready.

honey.en: having a golden-brown color like honey

ex.en: She has honey-colored hair.

honeymoon.en: a holiday taken by a couple just after they get married

ex.en: They went to Hawaii for their honeymoon.

honeymoon.en: a pleasant early period at the start of a relationship or new job

ex.en: The new manager's honeymoon period ended quickly.
ex.ko: 새 관리자의 허니문 기간은 빨리 끝났다.

honeymoon.en: go on a vacation after getting married

ex.en: They honeymooned in Hawaii.

humanist.en: a person who believes in human reason, values, and dignity rather than religious belief

ex.en: He was a humanist who valued reason over dogma.

humanist.en: relating to humanism, a philosophy that values human worth and reason

ex.en: The school follows a humanist approach to education.
ex.ko: 그 학교는 교육에서 인본주의적 접근법을 따른다.

ex.en: His speech was full of humor.

humor.en: the ability to understand and enjoy what is funny

ex.en: She has a great sense of humor.

humor.en: agree with someone or do what they want in order to keep them happy, even if you don't think it's necessary

ex.en: I just humored him and agreed to go.
ex.ko: 나는 그냥 그의 비위를 맞추며 가겠다고 했다.

hurt.en: cause pain or injury to someone

ex.en: I fell and hurt my knee.
Are you hurt?
The hurt in her eyes was obvious.
ex.ko: 그녀의 눈에는 마음의 상처가 분명히 드러났다.

ex.en: He felt too ill to go to work.
ex.ko: 그는 너무 아파서 출근할 수 없었다.

ill.en: in a bad, cruel, or unsatisfactory way

ex.en: The prisoners were ill-treated by the guards.

imitation.en: something made to look like the real thing, but not genuine

ex.en: The bag is an imitation, not the real designer brand.
ex.ko: 그 가방은 진짜 명품이 아니라 모조품이다.

imitation.en: the act of copying someone's actions, speech, or style

ex.en: Children learn language partly through imitation.
ex.ko: 아이들은 부분적으로 모방을 통해 언어를 배운다.

imitation.en: made to look like something real or genuine, but not authentic

ex.en: The necklace is made of imitation pearls.

impossible.cue: 할 수 없는, 일어날 수 없는
impossible.en: not able to happen, be done, or be true

ex.en: It's impossible to finish this in one day.
ex.ko: 이것을 하루 만에 끝내는 것은 불가능하다.

impossible.en: something that cannot happen or be done

ex.en: She always tries to achieve the impossible.
ex.ko: 그녀는 항상 불가능한 일을 이루려고 노력한다.

impressionist.en: a painter who works in the style of Impressionism

ex.en: The gallery displayed works by famous Impressionists.
ex.ko: 그 미술관은 유명한 인상주의 화가들의 작품을 전시했다.

impressionist.en: relating to Impressionism, an art style focused on light and color rather than detail

ex.en: The museum has a fine collection of impressionist paintings.
ex.ko: 그 박물관은 훌륭한 인상주의 회화 컬렉션을 보유하고 있다.

in.en: inside a place, area, or period of time

ex.en: The keys are in my bag.

in.en: a way of gaining special access or advantage, often through a personal connection (informal)

ex.en: He got the job because he had an in with the manager.
ex.ko: 그는 매니저와 연줄이 있어서 그 일자리를 얻었다.

incident.en: something that happens, especially something unusual or unpleasant

ex.en: The police were called to the scene of the incident.

incident.en: (of light or radiation) falling onto or striking a surface (technical, physics)

ex.en: The angle of the incident ray equals the angle of reflection.

inevitable.en: certain to happen and impossible to avoid

ex.en: Change is inevitable in a growing company.

inevitable.en: something that is certain to happen and cannot be avoided

ex.en: She finally accepted the inevitable and resigned.
ex.ko: 그녀는 결국 피할 수 없는 상황을 받아들이고 사임했다.

intermediate.en: at a level between beginner and advanced

ex.en: She is taking an intermediate Spanish class.
ex.ko: 그녀는 중급 스페인어 수업을 듣고 있다.

intermediate.en: a substance produced partway through a chemical reaction, before the final product forms

ex.en: The intermediate is unstable and reacts quickly.

iron.en: a strong, hard metal used to make steel and tools

ex.en: The gate is made of iron.

iron.en: a device used to make clothes smooth by pressing them

ex.en: Be careful, the iron is very hot.

iron.en: make clothes smooth using a heated iron

ex.en: She ironed her shirt before the interview.
She has an iron will and never gives up.
ex.ko: 그녀는 강철 같은 의지를 지녀서 결코 포기하지 않는다.

ex.en: He put on his jacket before going outside.
ex.ko: 그는 밖에 나가기 전에 재킷을 입었다.

jacket.en: cover something, such as a pipe, with insulating material

ex.en: The plumber jacketed the hot water pipes to save energy.
ex.ko: 배관공은 에너지를 절약하려고 온수 파이프를 단열재로 감쌌다.

jaw.cue: 입을 이루는 얼굴의 아래 뼈 부분
jaw.en: the bone structure that forms the mouth and holds the teeth

ex.en: He broke his jaw in the accident.

jaw.en: the part of a tool that opens and closes to grip something

ex.en: Place the metal between the jaws of the vice.
ex.ko: 금속을 바이스의 조 사이에 끼워 넣어라.

ex.en: The old friends sat jawing for hours.
ex.ko: 그 오랜 친구들은 몇 시간 동안 수다를 떨며 앉아 있었다.

jet.en: an airplane powered by jet engines

ex.en: The jet took off from the runway.

jet.en: travel somewhere quickly by jet plane

ex.en: She jetted off to Paris for the weekend.

journey.cue: 한 곳에서 다른 곳으로 가는 여행
journey.en: the act of traveling from one place to another

ex.en: The journey to the city took five hours.
ex.ko: 그 도시까지의 여정은 다섯 시간 걸렸다.

journey.en: travel from one place to another, especially over a long distance (literary)

ex.en: They journeyed across the desert for weeks.
ex.ko: 그들은 몇 주 동안 사막을 가로질러 여행했다.

junk.en: old or useless things that take up space

ex.en: The garage is full of junk.

junk.en: throw away or get rid of something that is no longer useful

ex.en: We junked the old computer last year.

just.cue: 그 정도밖에 안 됨을 나타낼 때

ex.en: It's just a small mistake.
I just finished my homework.
That's just what I wanted.
The judge made a just decision.

key.en: a small metal object used to lock or unlock something

ex.en: I forgot my house key at home.
ex.ko: 나는 집 열쇠를 집에 두고 왔다.

key.en: the most important thing needed for success or understanding

ex.en: Practice is the key factor in learning a language.
ex.ko: 연습이 언어 학습에서 가장 핵심적인 요소이다.

key.en: link or adjust something so that it matches or depends on something else

ex.en: The bonus is keyed to company profits.
ex.ko: 그 상여금은 회사 수익에 연동되어 있다.

kill.en: cause a person, animal, or plant to die

ex.en: The frost killed all the flowers in the garden.

kill.en: cause someone great pain or difficulty (informal)

ex.en: My feet are killing me after the long walk.

kill.en: an animal that has been killed, especially by a hunting predator

ex.en: The lion dragged its kill back to the pride.
ex.ko: 그 사자는 자신이 잡은 사냥감을 무리에게로 끌고 갔다.

knit.en: make clothing or fabric by looping yarn together with needles

ex.en: My grandmother knitted me a scarf.

knit.en: draw your eyebrows together, especially when worried or thinking hard

ex.en: He knitted his brows in confusion.

knit.en: a piece of clothing or fabric made by knitting

ex.en: She wore a cozy knit sweater.

knot.en: a fastening made by tying a piece of rope, string, or thread

ex.en: She tied a knot in the rope.

knot.en: a unit of speed used for ships and aircraft, equal to one nautical mile per hour

ex.en: The ship was sailing at 20 knots.
He knotted the rope around the post.

last.en: coming after all others; most recent

ex.en: This is the last chapter of the book.

last.en: continue for a period of time

ex.en: The meeting lasted two hours.
ex.ko: 그 회의는 두 시간 동안 지속되었다.

ex.en: When did you last see her?

last.en: the final part or moment of something

ex.en: We stayed until the very last of the concert.
ex.ko: 우리는 콘서트의 맨 마지막까지 남아 있었다.

lay.en: put something down carefully in a flat or horizontal position

ex.en: She laid the baby in the crib.
Hens lay eggs almost every day.

lay.en: not belonging to the clergy, or not a professional or expert in a particular field

ex.en: The book explains the law in terms a lay person can understand.
ex.ko: 그 책은 법을 일반인도 이해할 수 있는 말로 설명한다.

layer.en: a single thickness of material covering a surface or lying between other things

ex.en: The cake has three layers of chocolate.
ex.ko: 그 케이크는 세 겹의 초콜릿 층으로 되어 있다.

ex.en: She layered the cake with cream and strawberries.
ex.ko: 그녀는 케이크에 크림과 딸기를 층층이 쌓았다.

league.en: a group of sports teams that compete against each other

ex.en: His team won the league this season.
ex.ko: 그의 팀은 이번 시즌 리그에서 우승했다.

league.en: an association of countries, organizations, or people joined for a common purpose

ex.en: The League of Nations was formed after World War I.

league.en: join together with others to form an alliance or group

ex.en: The small companies leagued together to compete with larger firms.
ex.ko: 그 소규모 회사들은 대기업과 경쟁하기 위해 동맹을 맺었다.

lecture.en: a talk given to teach people about a subject

ex.en: The professor gave a lecture on climate change.
ex.ko: 그 교수는 기후 변화에 대해 강의했다.

lecture.en: a long talk in which someone criticizes or warns you about something

ex.en: I got a lecture from my mom about being late.
ex.ko: 나는 늦은 것에 대해 엄마한테 잔소리를 들었다.

lecture.en: give a formal talk on a subject, usually to a group of students

ex.en: She lectures on modern history at the university.

letter.en: a written message that you send to someone

ex.en: She wrote a letter to her grandmother.

letter.en: one of the symbols used to write a language, such as A or B

ex.en: The word 'cat' has three letters.

letter.en: write or mark letters on something

ex.en: The sign was lettered by hand.
ex.ko: 그 간판은 손으로 글자를 새겨 넣었다.

liberal.en: supporting social and political change and individual freedom

ex.en: Her parents have fairly liberal views on education.
ex.ko: 그녀의 부모님은 교육에 대해 꽤 진보적인 견해를 가지고 있다.

liberal.en: given in a large or generous amount

ex.en: He added a liberal amount of butter to the pan.

liberal.en: a person who supports political and social reform and progress

ex.en: The senator is known as a liberal on social issues.
ex.ko: 그 상원의원은 사회 문제에서 진보주의자로 알려져 있다.

ex.en: I like listening to music.

like.en: similar to or in the same way as something else

ex.en: She sings like a professional.
They shared like interests in music.
ex.ko: 그들은 음악에 대해 비슷한 관심사를 공유했다.

ex.en: It's likely to rain this afternoon.
ex.ko: 오늘 오후에 비가 올 것 같다.

ex.en: They will most likely arrive late.

little.en: not much; a small amount of something

ex.en: We have little time left.
They live in a little house by the lake.
Can I have a little of that cake?

local.en: relating to a particular area or neighborhood

ex.en: We shop at the local market.

local.en: a bus or train that stops at every station along its route

ex.en: We took the local instead of the express train.

log.en: a thick piece cut from the trunk of a tree

ex.en: They threw another log on the fire.
ex.ko: 그들은 불에 통나무를 하나 더 넣었다.

log.en: a written record of events, especially over time

ex.en: The captain kept a log of the ship's journey.

log.en: record information in an official record or log

ex.en: The pilot logged the flight details.
ex.ko: 조종사는 비행 세부 사항을 일지에 기록했다.

long.en: measuring a great distance from end to end

ex.en: She has long hair.

long.en: for a large amount of time

ex.en: We waited long for the bus.

long.en: want something very much, especially something you cannot easily have

ex.en: She longed for a quiet vacation.

loose.en: not firmly fixed or fitting tightly

ex.en: This shirt is too loose for me.

loose.en: set something or someone free from being tied or held

ex.en: They loosed the dogs to chase the rabbit.
The horse broke loose and ran across the field.
ex.ko: 그 말은 구속에서 풀려나 들판을 가로질러 달렸다.

lost.en: unable to find your way or know where you are

ex.en: We got lost in the old part of the city.
ex.ko: 우리는 도시의 옛 지역에서 길을 잃었다.

lost.en: no longer possessed, or unable to be found

ex.en: She never found her lost wallet.
ex.ko: 그녀는 잃어버린 지갑을 결국 찾지 못했다.

lost.en: people who are missing or have lost their way

ex.en: Rescue teams searched for the lost in the mountains.

lunch.en: the meal you eat in the middle of the day

ex.en: Let's have lunch together.
We lunched together at a small café.
ex.ko: 우리는 작은 카페에서 함께 점심을 먹었다.

major.en: an army officer of a rank above captain

ex.en: He was promoted to major last year.

major.en: the main subject that a student specializes in at college or university

ex.en: Her major is computer science.
Traffic is a major problem in this city.
ex.ko: 교통 체증은 이 도시의 주요한 문제이다.

major.en: study a particular subject as your main subject at college or university

ex.en: She majored in biology at college.

map.en: a drawing that shows the features of an area, such as roads or countries

ex.en: Can you show me on the map where we are?
ex.ko: 우리가 어디 있는지 지도에서 보여줄 수 있어?

ex.en: They mapped out their strategy for the next quarter.
ex.ko: 그들은 다음 분기 전략을 세밀하게 짰다.

mass.en: a large amount or number of something gathered together

ex.en: A mass of clouds covered the sky.

mass.en: the amount of physical matter an object has

ex.en: The mass of the object was measured in kilograms.
ex.ko: 그 물체의 질량은 킬로그램 단위로 측정되었다.

mass.en: involving a large number of people

ex.en: The concert drew mass attention from the media.
ex.ko: 그 콘서트는 언론의 대대적인 관심을 끌었다.

mass.en: gather together in a large group

ex.en: Troops massed along the border.

material.en: the substance or substances that something is made from

ex.en: This bag is made of recycled material.

material.en: information or ideas that can be used to create or complete something

ex.en: She collected material for her research paper.
ex.ko: 그녀는 연구 논문을 위한 자료를 수집했다.

material.en: important and relevant to a decision, especially in a legal case

ex.en: The lawyer presented material evidence to the court.
ex.ko: 그 변호사는 법정에 실질적인 증거를 제시했다.

meet.en: come together with someone, by chance or arrangement

ex.en: Let's meet at the cafe at noon.

meet.en: satisfy a need, requirement, or condition

ex.en: The product meets all safety standards.
ex.ko: 그 제품은 모든 안전 기준을 충족한다.

meet.en: an event where several sports competitions take place

ex.en: She qualified for the state track meet.
ex.ko: 그녀는 주 육상 경기 대회에 출전할 자격을 얻었다.

merchandise.en: goods that are bought and sold

ex.en: The store displays its merchandise in the window.

merchandise.en: promote or sell goods, often through advertising and displays

ex.en: The company merchandises its products through online ads.
ex.ko: 그 회사는 온라인 광고를 통해 제품을 판촉한다.

metal.en: a hard, shiny material such as iron, gold, or steel

ex.en: The bridge is made of metal.
He fixed the metal fence after the storm.
ex.ko: 그는 폭풍 이후 금속 울타리를 고쳤다.

meter.en: a metric unit of length equal to 100 centimeters

ex.en: The room is five meters wide.

meter.en: a device that measures and records the amount of something, such as gas or electricity

ex.en: He checked the electricity meter.

meter.en: measure the amount of something using a meter

ex.en: The utility company meters our water usage monthly.
ex.ko: 그 수도 회사는 매달 우리의 물 사용량을 계량기로 측정한다.

mighty.en: having great power, strength, or force

ex.en: The mighty river carved out the canyon over millions of years.
ex.ko: 그 강력한 강은 수백만 년에 걸쳐 협곡을 깎아냈다.

ex.en: That soup smells mighty good.

milk.en: a white liquid produced by cows and other animals, used as food or drink

ex.en: She drinks a glass of milk every morning.
ex.ko: 그녀는 매일 아침 우유 한 잔을 마신다.

milk.en: get as much benefit or advantage from something as possible, often unfairly

ex.en: He milked the situation for sympathy.
ex.ko: 그는 동정을 얻으려고 그 상황을 최대한 이용했다.

ex.en: The city has a population of two million.

million.en: amounting to a million in number

ex.en: The company donated a million dollars to charity.
ex.ko: 그 회사는 자선단체에 백만 달러를 기부했다.

mine.en: a place where minerals such as coal or gold are dug from the ground

ex.en: His grandfather worked in a coal mine.

mine.en: dig into the ground to get minerals such as coal or gold

ex.en: They mine coal in this region.

minister.en: a senior member of a government in charge of a particular department

ex.en: The Minister of Education announced new school policies.
ex.ko: 교육부 장관이 새로운 학교 정책을 발표했다.

minister.en: a person authorized to lead religious services, especially in a Christian church

ex.en: The minister gave a moving sermon.

minister.en: take care of someone's needs, especially when they are sick or in trouble

ex.en: Nurses ministered to the wounded soldiers.

minute.en: a unit of time equal to 60 seconds

ex.en: Wait a minute, please.

minute.cue: 눈에 잘 안 보일 만큼 아주 작은

ex.en: The scientist examined minute particles under the microscope.
ex.ko: 그 과학자는 현미경으로 미세한 입자들을 관찰했다.

moderate.en: average in amount, quality, or degree; not extreme

ex.en: He does a moderate amount of exercise every day.
ex.ko: 그는 매일 적당한 양의 운동을 한다.

moderate.en: (of opinions or policies) not extreme, especially in politics

ex.en: She has moderate political views.
ex.ko: 그녀는 온건한 정치적 견해를 가지고 있다.

moderate.en: lead a discussion or debate and make sure it stays fair and organized

ex.en: She will moderate tonight's debate.
ex.ko: 그녀는 오늘 밤 토론의 사회를 볼 것이다.

moderate.en: a person whose political views are not extreme, especially in the center of the spectrum

ex.en: Moderates on both sides pushed for a compromise.

modern.en: belonging to the present time or a recent time, rather than the past

ex.en: They live in a modern apartment.

modern.en: a person living in modern times (uncommon usage)

ex.en: The play contrasts ancient heroes with moderns.
ex.ko: 그 연극은 고대 영웅들과 현대인들을 대비시킨다.

more.en: a greater amount or degree of something

ex.en: Can I have more coffee, please?
ex.ko: 커피 좀 더 마실 수 있을까요?

more.en: a greater amount or number of something (comparative of much/many)

ex.en: We need more chairs for the guests.

mortal.en: certain to die at some point, as all living things are

ex.en: All humans are mortal.

mortal.en: causing or able to cause death

ex.en: He suffered a mortal wound in the battle.

mortal.cue: 신이 아닌 언젠가 죽는 존재로서의 인간
mortal.en: a human being, as distinguished from a god or immortal being

ex.en: Even kings are mortals in the end.

mortar.en: a mixture of cement, sand, and water used to hold bricks or stones together

ex.en: The bricklayer spread mortar between the bricks.

mortar.en: a short cannon that fires shells high into the air over a short distance

ex.en: The soldiers fired mortars at the enemy position.

mortar.en: join bricks or stones together using mortar

ex.en: The bricklayer mortared the wall carefully.
ex.ko: 그 벽돌공은 조심스럽게 벽에 회반죽을 발라 붙였다.
